seo.title: About — a small studio building an AI-native engine
seo.description: Geek Engine is a small Costa Rican studio building an AI-native game engine on Godot, so anyone can make a game by describing it instead of learning an engine first.

hero.heading: A small studio with one stubborn idea.
hero.sub: We think making a game should start with describing it, not with months of learning an engine. Geek Engine is our attempt to make that real — an AI-native game engine built on Godot, from a small team in Costa Rica.

sections.title: Why we're building this
body: Game engines are extraordinary, and they're also a wall. Between the idea in your head and a thing you can actually play sits a long climb: scene trees, scripting languages, build pipelines, export settings. Plenty of people with great game ideas never get over that wall.

Geek Engine exists to lower it. You describe the game you want — the world, the mechanics, the feel — and an AI does the engine work: editing a structured game model, generating the art and sound, and compiling a real build you can play in your browser. The learning curve stops being the price of admission.

sections.title: What we actually are
body: We're a small studio in Costa Rica, not a large company with a marketing department. We'd rather be honest about that than invent a roster of fake executives. The product is real and being built carefully; the team behind it is small and focused.

We build on Godot, the open-source engine, because it's genuinely good and because we don't believe creators should be locked into a black box. Your game compiles to a real Godot web build — not a toy runtime we made up.

sections.title: How we think about the work
body: AI-assisted doesn't mean AI-magic. Every change the system makes is structured, recorded as a snapshot, and inspectable, so you can always see what happened and roll it back. We'd rather the tool be honest and legible than impressively opaque.
We're pre-launch. That means we're choosing to ship something we trust rather than something we rushed. If you want to follow along, the launch newsletter is where the real updates land.

ctaStrip.heading: We're building in the open-ish.
ctaStrip.sub: Geek Engine isn't live yet. Subscribe for honest build notes and the first access window.
